senses.definition: Able to be attacked or criticized, especially without strong protection or defense.
examples: The city was highly attackable from the sea before the new walls were built.
Outdated software can make your computer more attackable by hackers.
His argument is attackable on several key points.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of attack: to have used force to hurt a person, animal, or place.
examples: The soldiers attacked the town at dawn.
He was attacked by a group of strangers on his way home.
Several ships were attacked during the night.

senses.definition: To have strongly criticized someone or something.
examples: The politician was attacked in the press for his comments.
Her ideas were attacked by several experts.
The report attacked the company’s safety record.

senses.definition: A person or animal that uses force to hurt someone or damage something.
examples: The attacker ran away before the police arrived.
Both attackers were caught on camera.
Her attacker has not yet been identified.

senses.definition: A player in a sport whose main job is to score points or goals.
examples: The attacker scored twice in the first half.
He plays as a wide attacker for the national team.
The coach replaced the defender with an extra attacker.

senses.definition: Plural of attacker: people or animals that use force to hurt someone or damage something.
examples: The attackers fled the scene in a car.
Several attackers were arrested after the incident.
Her attackers were later found guilty in court.

senses.definition: Present participle of attack: using force in order to hurt someone or damage something.
examples: The rebels are attacking the capital from the north.
The dog kept attacking anyone who came near the gate.
They were accused of attacking innocent civilians.

senses.definition: Present participle of attack: strongly criticizing someone or something.
examples: Commentators are attacking the government for the new law.
She is attacking the problem from a different angle.
The report is attacking the company’s environmental record.

senses.definition: Intended to move forward and try to score or win, rather than just defend.
examples: The team played very attacking football in the second half.
They adopted an attacking style from the start.
His attacking play caused problems for the defense.

senses.definition: In a way that attacks or seems ready to attack, physically or verbally.
examples: He glared at her attackingly across the table.
The critics wrote attackingly about his latest novel.

senses.definition: In lacrosse, a player whose main role is to score goals and play near the opponent’s goal.
examples: The attackman scored three goals in the final quarter.
As an attackman, he needs quick hands and good vision.
The coach moved him from midfield to attackman.

senses.definition: Plural of attackman: lacrosse players whose main role is to score goals and play near the opponent’s goal.
examples: The attackmen combined for six goals in the game.
Our attackmen are very fast and creative.
The coach praised the attackmen for their teamwork.

senses.definition: Third person singular of attack: uses force to hurt someone or to damage something.
examples: The lion usually attacks at night.
He often attacks his opponents personally.
The army attacks the city at dawn.

senses.definition: Plural of attack: acts of using force to hurt someone or damage something.
examples: There have been several attacks in the area recently.
The city has suffered heavy attacks from the air.
The police are investigating a series of attacks on tourists.

senses.definition: A mineral that is a hydrated phosphate of aluminum and manganese, usually found as light red masses.
examples: The geologist identified the sample as attacolite.
Attacolite occurs in small light red nodules in the region.
They collected pieces of attacolite for further study.

senses.definition: A genus of large moths, including the Atlas moth, found mainly in tropical Asia.
examples: Attacus includes some of the largest moths in the world.
They observed an Attacus species during the night survey.
The Atlas moth belongs to the genus Attacus.

senses.definition: Non-standard spelling of attagirl, used to praise or encourage a girl or woman.
examples: “Attagal, you did great out there!”
Her coach shouted, “Attagal, keep going!”
Parents on the side cheered, “Attagal, that’s the way!”

senses.definition: A sand grouse bird species, especially one found in parts of Asia and occasionally southern Europe.
examples: The hunters spotted an attagen near the lake.
An attagen flew low over the dry plain.
They recorded the call of an attagen during the survey.

senses.definition: A curved Turkish sword with a long single-edged blade, also called a yataghan.
examples: The museum displayed an old attaghan on the wall.
He drew his attaghan and stepped forward.
The attaghan had an ornate handle made of bone.

senses.definition: Used to praise or encourage a girl, woman, or female animal for doing something well.
examples: “Attagirl!” the coach shouted as she crossed the finish line.
You finished your homework on time—attagirl!
The trainer patted the dog and said, “Attagirl, good job.”

senses.definition: To succeed in getting or achieving something, especially after a lot of effort.
examples: She worked hard to attain her goals.
It may take years to attain fluency in a new language.
The company hopes to attain a higher market share.

senses.definition: To reach a particular level, age, or condition.
examples: He attained the age of 90.
Pollution levels have attained a dangerous point.
The tower attains a height of 100 meters.

senses.definition: Plural of attainability: the qualities of things that can be reached, achieved, or obtained.
examples: Students discussed the different attainabilities of their dreams.
We compared the attainabilities of several project targets.
The report questioned the attainabilities of the proposed goals.

senses.definition: The quality of being possible to reach or achieve.
examples: We need to consider the attainability of these targets.
They questioned the attainability of complete equality.
The plan’s attainability depends on the budget.

senses.definition: Possible to reach or achieve.
examples: Set goals that are challenging but attainable.
For many people, home ownership is no longer easily attainable.
Perfect accuracy may not be attainable in practice.

senses.definition: The state of being attainable; the possibility of being achieved.
examples: We discussed the attainableness of their ambitious plan.
The attainableness of such perfection is doubtful.
He stressed the attainableness of gradual change.

senses.definition: In a way that can be reached or achieved.
examples: The targets are attainably set for most students.
The price is attainably low for young families.
They described their goals as attainably realistic.

senses.definition: In historical law, the loss of civil rights and property after being sentenced to death or declared guilty of a serious crime.
examples: The law of attainder was used against political enemies.
He suffered attainder and lost his lands and title.
Parliament passed a bill of attainder against the rebel leader.

senses.definition: Plural of attainder: historical legal acts removing a person’s civil rights and property after conviction or sentence.
examples: Several attainders were issued during the conflict.
The king used attainders to punish his opponents.
Bills of attainders were later seen as unjust.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of attain: succeeded in achieving something.
examples: She attained her dream of becoming a doctor.
They finally attained their sales targets.
Only a few students attained the highest grade.

senses.definition: Reached a particular level, age, or condition.
examples: He attained the age of 80 in good health.
The temperature attained 40 degrees in the shade.
The tree has attained a height of 20 meters.

senses.definition: A person who attains something, especially a goal or level.
examples: High achievers are often early attainers of academic success.
She became an attainer of many personal goals at a young age.
The study compared early and late attainers in reading.

senses.definition: Plural of attainer: people who attain something, especially certain goals or levels.
examples: The class has both early and late attainers.
High attainers were given extra challenges.
Teachers must support both low and high attainers.

senses.definition: Present participle of attain: succeeding in achieving something.
examples: She is attaining a very high level of skill.
Attaining work–life balance can be difficult.
They are close to attaining their financial goals.

senses.definition: The act of achieving something, especially after effort.
examples: Her promotion marked the attainment of a long-held ambition.
The attainment of peace remains their main goal.
Educational attainment has improved in recent years.

senses.definition: A skill, quality, or level of knowledge that someone has gained.
examples: Languages were among his many attainments.
Her artistic attainments are widely admired.
The course will help you build new attainments.

senses.definition: Plural of attainment: achievements or skills that someone has gained.
examples: Her academic attainments are impressive.
They valued his musical attainments highly.
List your main attainments on your CV.

senses.definition: In historical law, a person who is declared guilty and suffers attainder.
examples: The attainor lost all his property to the crown.
Each attainor was named in the act.
Rights of the attainor were completely removed.

senses.definition: Third person singular of attain: succeeds in achieving something.
examples: If he works hard, he attains his goals.
The project rarely attains its full potential.
She usually attains excellent results.

senses.definition: In historical law, to declare someone guilty of a serious crime and remove their rights or property.
examples: The rebels were attainted by act of parliament.
He was attainted for treason.
Once attainted, a noble lost his title.

senses.definition: The state of dishonor or legal disqualification following such a declaration of guilt.
examples: He lived under the shadow of attaint.
The attaint of his name lasted for generations.
The king later removed the attaint from the family.

senses.definition: Having been legally declared guilty of a serious crime and having lost civil rights or property, especially in historical law.
examples: The attainted lord lost his castle and lands.
Attainted nobles were often executed.
He was an attainted traitor in the eyes of the crown.

senses.definition: Present participle of attaint: declaring someone guilty in law and removing their rights or property.
examples: Parliament was attainting its enemies without trial.
Attainting nobles became a tool of political control.
They opposed attainting citizens by special acts.

senses.definition: The action or result of attainting someone, especially the legal loss of rights and property after conviction.
examples: The attaintment of the rebel leaders shocked the court.
Attaintment often led to the loss of family lands.
They protested against the use of attaintment for political revenge.

senses.definition: Plural of attaintment: legal acts removing the rights and property of people found guilty, especially in historical law.
examples: Many attaintments were later reversed.
The king ordered several attaintments in one year.
These attaintments targeted his political rivals.

senses.definition: Third person singular of attaint: declares someone guilty and removes their rights or property in historical law.
examples: Parliament attaints the rebel without a trial.
The act attaints all his heirs.
The crown attaints those who oppose it.

senses.definition: Plural of attaint: states of legal or social disgrace after such condemnation.
examples: Old attaints still stained the family name.
These attaints were remembered for generations.
The king removed several attaints after the war.

senses.definition: An obsolete term meaning the condition of being found guilty of an offense, or the resulting dishonor.
examples: He lived under attainture after the failed rebellion.
The family’s attainture was never fully removed.
Writers of the time described the moral attainture of the court.

senses.definition: Plural of attainture: obsolete term for conditions of guilt, dishonor, or legal condemnation.
examples: Old attaintures weighed heavily on the noble houses.
The new king removed several attaintures.
Political conflicts left many attaintures unresolved.

senses.definition: Obsolete form meaning unavoidable or impossible to avoid.
examples: They accepted the attal outcome with calm.
War seemed almost attal at that point.
He spoke of the attal march of time.

senses.definition: A genus of palm trees native to Mexico, the Caribbean, Central America, and South America.
examples: Attalea species are common in parts of the Amazon.
They studied the fruit of Attalea palms.
Attalea has been used as a source of palm oil.

senses.definition: A rare or obsolete variant name for a type of palm or palm product.
examples: The trader sold fiber made from attaleh.
Attaleh was once exported from the region.
They wrote about attaleh in early travel accounts.

senses.definition: Relating to the Attalid dynasty that ruled the ancient kingdom of Pergamon.
examples: They studied Attalid art and architecture.
The Attalid kings were famous for their libraries.
Attalid culture mixed Greek and local traditions.

senses.definition: A member of the Attalid royal family.
examples: An Attalid ruled the city at that time.
Several Attalids supported the arts.
The Attalids were allies of Rome.

senses.definition: Obsolete form of attempt or undertake.
examples: He dared not attame such a task.
Few would attame a journey through the wild forest.
They chose to attame the dangerous climb.

senses.definition: Leaves of certain palm trees used for thatching roofs in parts of Southeast Asia.
examples: The hut was covered with attap.
They repaired the roof using fresh attap.
Attap keeps the house cool in hot weather.

senses.definition: Plural of attap: leaves of palm trees used as roof thatch.
examples: They stacked attaps by the side of the house.
New attaps were brought in after the storm.
The villagers dried the attaps in the sun.

senses.definition: A type of clay mineral used in products such as absorbents, drilling mud, and some medicines.
examples: The cat litter was made with attapulgite.
Attapulgite is often used as an absorbent clay.
They studied the properties of attapulgite in the lab.

senses.definition: A strong natural perfume, especially made from the essential oil of flowers such as roses.
examples: She dabbed a little rose attar on her wrists.
The market was full of the smell of attar.
He bought a small bottle of jasmine attar.

senses.definition: Variant form of attar of rose; a fragrant oil or perfume made from rose petals.
examples: The shop specialized in attargul and other oils.
She used a drop of attargul each morning.
Attargul is highly valued for its delicate scent.

senses.definition: Plural of attar: natural perfumes made from flower oils.
examples: The shop sells many different attars.
Traditional attars are often stored in small glass bottles.
She prefers attars to synthetic perfumes.
